zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Wood於1514年6月20日出生在英國時，他的父親John Woods24歲，母親Joanne Strowde23歲。他於1524年與Elizabeth Story在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1588年9月30日享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾哈利法可。

nameMeanings.name: Wood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: mainly a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a wood, from Middle English wode ‘wood’ (Old English wudu). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, such as French Bois and Polish Les .
English: in a few cases, a nickname for an eccentric or perhaps a violent person, from Middle English wode ‘frenzied, wild’ (Old English wōd).

variants.name: Woode
meanings: English: variant of   Wood  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 312, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Woods
meanings: English: variant of Wood with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish: adopted as a translation of Ó Cuill ‘descendant of Coll’ (see Quill ), or in Ulster of Mac Con Coille ‘son of Cú Choille’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the wood’, which has also been mistranslated Cox , as if formed with coileach ‘cock, rooster’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Wood was born on 20 June 1514, in England, his father, John Woods, was 24 and his mother, Joanne Strowde, was 23. He married Elizabeth Story in 1524, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 30 September 1588, at the age of 74, and was buried in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Wood est né le 20 juin 1514, à Angleterre, son père, John Woods, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Joanne Strowde, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Story en 1524, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 30 septembre 1588, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Woodが1514年6月20日にイギリスで生まれた時，父John Woodsは24歳，母Joanne Strowdeは23歳であった。1524年にイギリスイギリスでElizabeth Storyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1588年9月30日に74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Wood(이)가 1514년 6월 20일 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Woods(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Joanne Strowde(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1524년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Elizabeth Story(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1588년 9월 30일 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 할이파스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Wood родился 20 июня 1514 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John Woods было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Joanne Strowde, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Story в 1524 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 30 сентября 1588 г. в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
